_comment_variant: HYDRA Variant C — Brandon Trojan Horse stack with Brandon's narrow 5/10pt spread widths (his actual rule from the interview). Same Brandon feature stack as variant B; only spread width differs. Dry-run for the first 4 weeks. After observation: B vs C tells us whether narrow widths help on top of Brandon's additions.

strategy._comment_widths: MKT-027 settings here are unused at runtime — strategy.brandon.narrow_spread.enabled=true overrides _get_vix_adjusted_spread_width with Brandon's 5/10pt rule. Values kept aligned with variant A's VM config (max=75pt) for consistency / debugging clarity.
strategy._comment_put_only_max_vix: Lifted from HYDRA-baseline 15 → 28 because Brandon GEX-ADJ skip-and-route-as-put-only is a core part of his strategy when SPX is inside the call accel zone — capping the fallback at VIX < 15 would block it on most days (VIX 15-25 is normal, not extreme).
strategy._comment_credit_floors: Lowered ~10× vs variants A/B because narrow_spread.enabled=true makes the IC 5pt or 10pt wide instead of 75pt — credits scale roughly proportionally with spread width, so HYDRA's standard $0.20/$0.30 floors would skip every entry.
strategy._comment_safety_caps: ORDER-006 caps raised vs 1c default (10 / 30) to accommodate Brandon narrow-spread sizing: 15c × 4 entries × 4 legs = 240 worst-case open contracts on SPX. 20 per-order cap leaves headroom for size adjustments without disabling the bug-protection check entirely.
strategy._comment_downday_callonly: OFF on C — Brandon GEX adjuster is the sole side-selection logic on the Brandon stack; HYDRA's down-day call-only override would conflict with GEX zone reads.
strategy._comment_conditional_directional: OFF on C — directional bias (down-day put-only at 14:00 / up-day put-only at 14:00) is HYDRA's heuristic. Brandon GEX adjuster already makes the side-selection call from gamma zones; layering both produces conflicting signals on mixed days.

vix_regime._comment_min_credits: Per-VIX-zone credit gates also lowered ~10× because narrow 5/10pt spreads collect $0.05-$0.20 typical at 8-delta vs A/B's $1.00-$2.00 on 75pt-wide spreads. Without this scaling, MKT-011 skips every C entry by design (verified live 2026-05-05 10:45: C reported call $0.08 / put $0.02 vs A/B's $1.15 / $1.23 on the same chain).

directional_pivot._comment: Disabled in variant C — Brandon GEX-breach exit (shadow) plays the same role.

brandon._comment: Identical Brandon stack to variant B; only narrow_spread.enabled differs. Per Brandon: 5pt at VIX < 22, 10pt at VIX >= 22 (plus a 25pt safety ceiling). All other Brandon features are fully LIVE (TP / strike adjuster / breach exit / overlay). Only shadow behavior is hydra_stop_shadow — same as variant B.

hydra_stop_shadow._comment: When true, computes HYDRA's existing credit+buffer stop every tick in parallel without acting. First fire per side per day is Telegrammed for head-to-head comparison against Brandon's GEX breach (which IS the live stop in B/C).

google_sheets._comment: Top-level google_sheets block — schema GoogleSheetsLogger.__init__ actually reads. The logging.* keys are read by LocalFileLogger and a few other consumers, but Sheets-specific config lives here. Mirrors variant A's block, only spreadsheet_name differs.
